heading: Wages on discharge when vessel sold
label: 11105 Wages on discharge when vessel sold
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)1110546:684Section 11105 provides that when a United States vessel is sold in a foreign port, the seamen must be provided employment on another vessel, or passage to the port of original shipment.

children.text: provide the seaman with the means to return to the port of original engagement;
provide the seaman passage to the port of original engagement; or
deposit with the consular officer an amount of money considered sufficient by the officer to provide the seaman with maintenance and passage home.

children.label: b
children.text: The consular officer shall endorse on the agreement the particulars of the payment, provision, or deposit made under this section.

children.label: c
children.text: An owner of a vessel is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of $500 if the master does not comply with this section.
